evals.name: parse-well-formed-cdc-err
evals.prompt:
A tool I invoked returned this body in its tool_result:

```json
{
  "type": "https://api.example.com/errors/rate-limit-exceeded",
  "title": "Rate limit exceeded",
  "status": 429,
  "detail": "Exceeded 100 requests/minute on /v1/embed.",
  "instance": "urn:request:2026-05-21T14:22:10Z-req-abc123",
  "retry_after": 180,
  "suggested_fix": { "value": "Wait 180 seconds before retrying.", "applicability": "machine_applicable" },
  "code_actions": [{ "kind": "retry", "title": "Retry after 180 seconds", "applicability": "machine_applicable", "args": { "delay_s": 180 } }]
}
```

Parse this — just tell me what is in the envelope. Do not propose an action. Save your answer to answer.md.
evals.expected_output: Parse-mode output naming envelope_kind=cdc-err, status_class=transient, the type URI, the retry_after value, the suggested_fix value plus applicability, the code_actions entry plus its kind and applicability, and no malformations.
expectations: Identifies envelope_kind as cdc-err
Classifies status_class as transient (429)
Names retry_after with value 180
Names suggested_fix.applicability as machine_applicable
Lists at least one code_action with kind=retry and applicability=machine_applicable
Does not propose a decision or action (Parse mode only)

deterministic_checks.description: Output identifies itself as parse-only (no decision)

evals.name: parse-malformed-missing-extensions
evals.prompt:
I got this back from a CLI in a tool_result:

```json
{
  "type": "https://api.example.com/errors/upstream-unavailable",
  "title": "Upstream unavailable",
  "status": 503,
  "detail": "The embedding service is unreachable.",
  "instance": "urn:request:2026-05-21T14:23:00Z-req-def456"
}
```

Is this a cdc-err envelope? What's wrong with it? Save your answer to answer.md.
evals.expected_output: Identifies the body as RFC 9457 but NOT cdc-err-shaped (all three mandatory extensions absent). Status class transient (503). Lists at least two malformations: missing retry_after on a transient status, and all three mandatory extensions absent.
expectations: States the envelope is RFC 9457 but not cdc-err-shaped
Classifies status_class as transient (503)
Flags that retry_after is absent on a transient status
Flags that all three mandatory cdc-err extensions are absent
Names the malformations explicitly rather than treating the envelope as well-formed

deterministic_checks.description: States envelope is RFC 9457 but not cdc-err

evals.name: decide-429-with-retry-after
evals.prompt:
Should I retry? The tool returned:

```json
{
  "type": "https://api.example.com/errors/rate-limit-exceeded",
  "title": "Rate limit exceeded",
  "status": 429,
  "detail": "Exceeded rate limit.",
  "instance": "urn:request:abc",
  "retry_after": 30,
  "suggested_fix": null,
  "code_actions": []
}
```

Give me your decision. Save your answer to answer.md.
evals.expected_output: Decision = retry. Reason cites status 429 + retry_after present. Plan empty (Decide mode only) or a single retry step with delay_s=30.
expectations: Decision is retry
Reason mentions 429 / transient class
Reason mentions retry_after present
The delay value 30 is named
Does not invent a backoff that contradicts retry_after

evals.name: decide-503-without-retry-after
evals.prompt:
Should I retry?

```json
{
  "type": "https://api.example.com/errors/upstream-down",
  "title": "Upstream down",
  "status": 503,
  "detail": "Service unavailable.",
  "instance": "urn:request:xyz",
  "retry_after": null,
  "suggested_fix": null,
  "code_actions": []
}
```

Save your answer to answer.md.
evals.expected_output: Decision = handback. Reason: 503 (transient) with retry_after null/absent — an agent guessing a backoff is what cdc-err exists to prevent.
expectations: Decision is handback (NOT retry)
Reason cites 503 / transient with retry_after null or absent
Reason explicitly says do not guess a backoff
Output does not propose a retry with an invented delay

deterministic_checks.description: Explicitly refuses to guess a backoff

evals.name: decide-client-error-machine-applicable
evals.prompt:
Decision?

```json
{
  "type": "https://cli.example.com/errors/missing-env",
  "title": "Missing env",
  "status": 422,
  "detail": "EMBED_API_KEY is required.",
  "instance": "urn:request:p",
  "retry_after": null,
  "suggested_fix": { "value": "export EMBED_API_KEY=$(get-key)", "applicability": "machine_applicable" },
  "code_actions": [{ "kind": "env-set", "applicability": "machine_applicable", "args": { "name": "EMBED_API_KEY", "value": "<from-vault>" } }]
}
```

Save your answer to answer.md.
evals.expected_output: Decision = apply_fix. Reason: client_error (422) with machine_applicable applicability on both suggested_fix and the env-set code_action.
expectations: Decision is apply_fix
Reason cites client_error / 422
Reason explicitly notes machine_applicable applicability
References env-set as the code_action kind
Does not propose retry on a 422

deterministic_checks.description: References the env var from the envelope

evals.name: decide-client-error-maybe-incorrect
evals.prompt:
Decision?

```json
{
  "type": "https://cli.example.com/errors/bad-flag",
  "title": "Bad flag",
  "status": 400,
  "detail": "Unknown option --threshold.",
  "instance": "urn:request:q",
  "retry_after": null,
  "suggested_fix": { "value": "Did you mean --max?", "applicability": "maybe_incorrect" },
  "code_actions": []
}
```

Save your answer to answer.md.
evals.expected_output: Decision = handback. Reason: 400 client error with applicability maybe_incorrect — the applicability gate has not been cleared. Suggested fix is surfaced as a proposed action, not auto-applied.
expectations: Decision is handback (NOT apply_fix)
Reason cites maybe_incorrect applicability
Output explicitly states the fix is NOT auto-applied
Surfaces the proposed fix to the human (does not silently drop it)

deterministic_checks.description: Says fix is not auto-applied / is handed back
Names the proposed flag or symbol from the envelope

evals.name: decide-has-placeholders
evals.prompt:
Decision?

```json
{
  "type": "https://cli.example.com/errors/missing-dep",
  "title": "Missing dependency",
  "status": 422,
  "detail": "Package <name> is required.",
  "instance": "urn:request:r",
  "retry_after": null,
  "suggested_fix": { "value": "Run: pip install <name>", "applicability": "has_placeholders" },
  "code_actions": [{ "kind": "exec", "applicability": "has_placeholders", "args": { "command": "pip install <name>" } }]
}
```

Save your answer to answer.md.
evals.expected_output: Decision = handback. Reason: has_placeholders applicability — the fix template needs a value for <name>. Output enumerates <name> as a placeholder.
expectations: Decision is handback
Reason cites has_placeholders
Output names <name> as the placeholder to be filled
Output does NOT auto-apply the fix
Output does NOT autonomously substitute a guessed value for <name>

deterministic_checks.description: References the command shape from the envelope

evals.name: decide-applicability-absent
evals.prompt:
Decision?

```json
{
  "type": "https://cli.example.com/errors/bad-config",
  "title": "Bad config",
  "status": 422,
  "detail": "timeout must be > 0.",
  "instance": "urn:request:s",
  "retry_after": null,
  "suggested_fix": { "value": "Set timeout to 30." },
  "code_actions": []
}
```

Note that suggested_fix has no applicability field. Save your answer to answer.md.
evals.expected_output: Decision = handback. Reason: applicability is absent — treated as unspecified — treated as maybe_incorrect — do not auto-apply. Output records this as a malformation as well.
expectations: Decision is handback
Identifies that applicability is absent on suggested_fix
States the absent-applicability rule (treated as unspecified / maybe_incorrect)
Does NOT auto-apply the fix
Records the absent applicability as a malformation

deterministic_checks.description: Records the absent-applicability as a malformation

evals.name: act-retry-plan
evals.prompt:
I want you to produce an Act-mode plan. The envelope:

```json
{
  "type": "https://api.example.com/errors/rate-limit-exceeded",
  "title": "Rate limit exceeded",
  "status": 429,
  "detail": "Exceeded rate limit.",
  "instance": "urn:request:tt",
  "retry_after": 60,
  "suggested_fix": { "value": "Wait 60s.", "applicability": "machine_applicable" },
  "code_actions": [{ "kind": "retry", "applicability": "machine_applicable", "args": { "delay_s": 60 } }]
}
```

Produce the executable action plan as JSON. Save it to answer.md.
evals.expected_output: An executable plan with decision=retry, one plan step of kind=retry, applicability_gate=machine_applicable, args.delay_s=60, on_failure=handback, and envelope_refs naming status=429 and retry_after=60.
expectations: Output contains a JSON plan with a decision field equal to retry
Plan has at least one step with kind retry
Plan step references delay_s=60
Plan step records applicability_gate=machine_applicable
Plan step has an on_failure field (typically handback)
envelope_refs surfaces status=429 and retry_after=60

evals.name: act-handback-plan
evals.prompt:
Produce an Act-mode plan for this envelope:

```json
{
  "type": "https://cli.example.com/errors/missing-config",
  "title": "Missing config",
  "status": 422,
  "detail": "config.yml has no apiToken.",
  "instance": "urn:request:uu",
  "retry_after": null,
  "suggested_fix": { "value": "Add apiToken to config.yml.", "applicability": "maybe_incorrect" },
  "code_actions": []
}
```

Save to answer.md.
evals.expected_output: An executable plan with decision=handback, one plan step of kind=handback with action=emit_handback_to_user, the proposed fix surfaced verbatim in args.
expectations: Plan has decision=handback (not apply_fix or retry)
Plan step has kind=handback
Plan step describes the proposed fix without applying it
The applicability_gate is maybe_incorrect
No file edit or shell command is run

deterministic_checks.description: Action emits handback rather than running a command/edit

evals.name: act-unknown-kind-handback
evals.prompt:
Produce an Act plan. The code_action has an unfamiliar kind:

```json
{
  "type": "https://cli.example.com/errors/credential-rotation",
  "title": "Credential rotation needed",
  "status": 500,
  "detail": "Vault credentials expired.",
  "instance": "urn:request:zz",
  "retry_after": null,
  "suggested_fix": null,
  "code_actions": [{ "kind": "rotate-credentials", "applicability": "machine_applicable", "args": { "vault": "prod" } }]
}
```

Save to answer.md.
evals.expected_output: Decision = handback even though applicability is machine_applicable, because the kind 'rotate-credentials' is not recognized. Plan surfaces the original code_action verbatim and asks the human to confirm. Skill does NOT invent semantics for the unknown kind.
expectations: Decision is handback (NOT apply_fix)
Plan names the unknown kind 'rotate-credentials' verbatim
Plan explicitly says the kind is not recognized
Original code_action is included verbatim in the handback args
Skill does not invent a tool call for rotate-credentials

deterministic_checks.description: Surfaces the applicability marker from the envelope
Surfaces the args.vault value from the envelope

evals.name: handoff-to-cdc-err
evals.prompt: I'm designing my CLI's error output. What fields should the problem+json envelope contain? Save your answer to answer.md.
evals.expected_output: Skill recognizes this is a producer-side question and defers to cdc-err. It names cdc-err explicitly and does not attempt to enumerate the envelope schema itself.
expectations: Output names cdc-err as the sibling skill to consult
Output states the current question is producer-side / out of scope for cdc-handle
Output does NOT enumerate the full envelope schema itself

deterministic_checks.description: States this is a producer/out-of-scope question

evals.name: handoff-to-cdc-review
evals.prompt: The CLI that's emitting these problem+json errors has bad error-propagation upstream — it's swallowing exceptions before they reach the formatter. Review the code. Save your answer to answer.md.
evals.expected_output: Skill recognizes this is a source-code propagation question and defers to cdc-review. It names cdc-review explicitly and does not attempt the code review itself.
expectations: Output names cdc-review as the sibling skill to consult
Output states the current question is source-code propagation / out of scope for cdc-handle
Output does NOT attempt to review the propagation itself

deterministic_checks.description: States this is a propagation/out-of-scope question
Identifies the question as about source-code error-handling

evals.name: out-of-scope-raw-stderr
evals.prompt:
The tool spat out this plain stderr:

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "app.py", line 12, in <module>
    fetch(sys.argv[1])
  File "app.py", line 7, in fetch
    r.raise_for_status()
requests.exceptions.HTTPError: 503 Server Error
```

What should I do? Save your answer to answer.md.
evals.expected_output: Skill recognizes this is not a problem+json envelope. It says so explicitly, declines to act with the cdc-handle decision lattice, and either hands back to the user or recommends a more general error-reader / asks for an actual envelope.
expectations: Output states explicitly that this is not a problem+json envelope / not RFC 9457
Output declines to produce a Parse/Decide/Act response based on this payload
Output does NOT invent a decision (e.g. does not silently emit decision=retry)
Output suggests the user obtain an actual envelope or use a different tool

deterministic_checks.description: Identifies the payload as not-an-envelope or raw stderr
Declines to act on a non-envelope
Names the envelope contract this skill expects

evals.name: decide-precedence-stricter-marker-wins
evals.prompt:
Decision? Note that suggested_fix and code_actions[0] disagree on applicability:

```json
{
  "type": "https://cli.example.com/errors/bad-input",
  "title": "Bad input",
  "status": 422,
  "detail": "--threshold must be int.",
  "instance": "urn:request:vv",
  "retry_after": null,
  "suggested_fix": { "value": "Pass --threshold=5", "applicability": "machine_applicable" },
  "code_actions": [{ "kind": "exec", "applicability": "maybe_incorrect", "args": { "command": "yourcli --threshold=5" } }]
}
```

Save your answer to answer.md.
evals.expected_output: Decision = handback. Reason: applicability disagreement between suggested_fix (machine_applicable) and code_actions[0] (maybe_incorrect) — the stricter marker wins per the safety rule. Output explains the precedence rule.
expectations: Decision is handback
Reason notes that suggested_fix and code_actions[0] disagree on applicability
Output explicitly states the stricter marker wins (or 'most cautious wins')
Output does NOT auto-apply based on the more-permissive suggested_fix

deterministic_checks.description: Names the more-permissive marker that loses
